commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the branch still has P1 compatibility/security blockers and the external contributor must supply real compaction proof from their setup.; Needs attention: The diff improves structured credential redaction but still leaves a concrete raw-text secret extraction path in the expanded transcript-wide identifier scanner.; Review finding: [P1] Honor the quality guard opt-out before ca... (sha=aa1031ac9d6f5c49da1aefdc3f9742b482c51698)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds transcript-wide compaction identifier extraction and survival checks, credential redaction before identifier extraction, strict post-cap cancellation, and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +212, Tests +624. Total +836 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main honors qualityGuard.enabled:false before quality audits, while the PRâ€™s strict post-cap cancellation branch does not check that opt-out; raw text also flows into identifier extraction before only prefix-based redaction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the branch still has P1 compatibility/security blockers and the external contributor must supply real compaction proof from their setup.

target.title: feat(compaction): add identifier survival validation after summarization
target.body:
Fixes #90010
Fixes #79588

## Summary

Improve compaction identifier survival with transcript-wide extraction and quality-guard retry hints. Fixes two security/correctness issues found during review:

**Fix 1 â€” Sanitize string-encoded JSON arguments before extraction**: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` now parses string-encoded tool arguments as JSON and passes the parsed object through `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before identifier scanning. Previously, string arguments (e.g. OpenAI's JSON-stringified format) were appended raw, allowing credential values like `{"token":"deadbeef12345678"}` to feed the hex identifier extractor and leak into `## Exact identifiers`.

**Fix 2 â€” Fail strict compaction when cap recovery cannot fit**: When strict mode detects that final capping removed required identifiers and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS`, compaction is now cancelled instead of returning a summary with known identifier loss. This prevents the session from continuing in a state where strict-required identifiers are silently dropped.

## Changed files

- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` â€” Parse string args as JSON, sanitize via `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before appending; non-JSON strings still pass through
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` â€” Cancel compaction (return `{ cancel: true }` with reason) when strict cap recovery overflows instead of silently skipping
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` â€” Three regression tests: string-encoded JSON credential sanitization, non-JSON passthrough, strict-mode cap overflow cancel

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Two security/correctness issues in compaction identifier survival. (1) `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` appended string-encoded tool arguments raw, allowing credential values like `{"token":"deadbeef12345678"}` to feed the hex identifier extractor and leak into `## Exact identifiers`. (2) When strict mode detected that final capping removed required identifiers and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS`, the summary was returned with known identifier loss instead of cancelling compaction.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5 arm64, Node v22.19.0, pnpm v11.2.2. Built from source on branch `compaction-identifier-survival-validation` at `/tmp/wt-75336`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Create proof script exercising production `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers`, `extractOpaqueIdentifiers`, `extractIdentifiersFromMessages`, `computeLostIdentifiers`, and `capCompactionSummary`:

```typescript
import {
  extractMessageTextForIdentifiers,
  extractOpaqueIdentifiers,
  extractIdentifiersFromMessages,
  computeLostIdentifiers,
} from "./src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.js";
import { testing } from "./src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.js";
const { capCompactionSummary, MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS } = testing;
```

Step 2. Validate Fix 1, string-encoded JSON args with credential `token` field:

```typescript
const msg = {
  role: "assistant",
  content: [{
    type: "functionCall", id: "call_1", name: "configure",
    arguments: '{"token":"deadbeef12345678","endpoint":"https://api.acme.io/v2"}',
  }],
};
const text = extractMessageTextForIdentifiers(msg);
assert(!text.includes("deadbeef12345678"));    // credential redacted
assert(text.includes("https://api.acme.io/v2")); // non-credential survives
```

Step 3. Validate Fix 2, capping loses identifiers and recovery overflows:

```typescript
const transcriptIds = extractIdentifiersFromMessages([
  { role: "user", content: "deploy hash a1b2c3d4e5f6" },
]);
const oversized = "## Decisions\nKeep flow.\n## Exact identifiers\n"
  + "x".repeat(MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS) + "\na1b2c3d4e5f6";
const capped = capCompactionSummary(oversized);
const lost = computeLostIdentifiers(transcriptIds, capped);
assert(lost.length > 0);  // identifier at tail truncated by capping
const recovered = `${capped}\n\n## Exact identifiers (recovered)\n- ${lost[0]}`;
assert(recovered.length > MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS); // recovery overflows
```

Step 4. Run `npx tsx proof-75336.ts` from the patched branch.

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ cd /tmp/wt-75336 && npx tsx proof-75336.ts

[Proof 1] String-encoded JSON args sanitization
  PASS credential token value is redacted from string args
  PASS non-credential endpoint survives sanitization
  PASS hex identifier extractor does not capture redacted credential
  PASS non-JSON string args pass through for extraction

[Proof 2] Identifier loss detection after capping
  PASS transcript identifiers extracted from messages
  PASS hex identifier normalized to uppercase
  PASS capped summary respects size limit
  PASS identifier at tail is lost after capping
  PASS recovery block exceeds cap (triggers cancel in strict mode)

Results: 9 passed, 0 failed
```

Observed result after fix:

Fix 1: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` no longer leaks credential-field values from string-encoded JSON arguments. The `token` field value `deadbeef12345678` is redacted by `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before the hex identifier extractor can capture it. Non-credential fields and non-JSON strings pass through normally.

Fix 2: When strict mode detects identifier loss after capping and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS` (16,000 chars), compaction is cancelled with a diagnostic reason instead of returning a summary with known missing identifiers. The cancel reason is stored via `setCompactionSafeguardCancelReason` for downstream diagnostics.

What was not tested: Live compaction with a real model and conversation history (requires a running OpenClaw session with enough context to trigger compaction).
target.author: sebtardif

```console
  PASS non-credential endpoint survives sanitization
  PASS hex identifier extractor does not capture redacted credential
  PASS non-JSON string args pass through for extraction

[Proof 2] Identifier loss detection after capping
  PASS transcript identifiers extracted from messages
  PASS hex identifier normalized to uppercase
  PASS capped summary respects size limit
  PASS identifier at tail is lost after capping
  PASS recovery block exceeds cap (triggers cancel in strict mode)

Results: 9 passed, 0 failed
```

Observed result after fix:

Fix 1: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` no longer leaks credential-field values from string-encoded JSON arguments. The `token` field value `deadbeef12345678` is redacted by `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before the hex identifier extractor can capture it. Non-credential fields and non-JSON strings pass through normally.

Fix 2: When strict mode detects identifier loss after capping and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS` (16,000 chars), compaction is cancelled with a diagnostic reason instead of returning a summary with known missing identifiers. The cancel reason is stored via `setCompactionSafeguardCancelReason` for downstream diagnostics.

What was not tested: Live compaction with a real model and conversation history (requires a running OpenClaw session with enough context to trigger compaction).

commands.author: SebTardif

target.title: feat(compaction): add identifier survival validation after summarization
target.body:
Fixes #90010
Fixes #79588

## Summary

Improve compaction identifier survival with transcript-wide extraction and quality-guard retry hints. Fixes two security/correctness issues found during review:

**Fix 1 â€” Sanitize string-encoded JSON arguments before extraction**: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` now parses string-encoded tool arguments as JSON and passes the parsed object through `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before identifier scanning. Previously, string arguments (e.g. OpenAI's JSON-stringified format) were appended raw, allowing credential values like `{"token":"deadbeef12345678"}` to feed the hex identifier extractor and leak into `## Exact identifiers`.

**Fix 2 â€” Fail strict compaction when cap recovery cannot fit**: When strict mode detects that final capping removed required identifiers and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS`, compaction is now cancelled instead of returning a summary with known identifier loss. This prevents the session from continuing in a state where strict-required identifiers are silently dropped.

## Changed files

- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` â€” Parse string args as JSON, sanitize via `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before appending; non-JSON strings still pass through
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` â€” Cancel compaction (return `{ cancel: true }` with reason) when strict cap recovery overflows instead of silently skipping
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` â€” Three regression tests: string-encoded JSON credential sanitization, non-JSON passthrough, strict-mode cap overflow cancel

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Two security/correctness issues in compaction identifier survival. (1) `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` appended string-encoded tool arguments raw, allowing credential values like `{"token":"deadbeef12345678"}` to feed the hex identifier extractor and leak into `## Exact identifiers`. (2) When strict mode detected that final capping removed required identifiers and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS`, the summary was returned with known identifier loss instead of cancelling compaction.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5 arm64, Node v22.19.0, pnpm v11.2.2. Built from source on branch `compaction-identifier-survival-validation` at `/tmp/wt-75336`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Create proof script exercising production `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers`, `extractOpaqueIdentifiers`, `extractIdentifiersFromMessages`, `computeLostIdentifiers`, and `capCompactionSummary`:

```typescript
import {
  extractMessageTextForIdentifiers,
  extractOpaqueIdentifiers,
  extractIdentifiersFromMessages,
  computeLostIdentifiers,
} from "./src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.js";
import { testing } from "./src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.js";
const { capCompactionSummary, MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS } = testing;
```

Step 2. Validate Fix 1, string-encoded JSON args with credential `token` field:

```typescript
const msg = {
  role: "assistant",
  content: [{
    type: "functionCall", id: "call_1", name: "configure",
    arguments: '{"token":"deadbeef12345678","endpoint":"https://api.acme.io/v2"}',
  }],
};
const text = extractMessageTextForIdentifiers(msg);
assert(!text.includes("deadbeef12345678"));    // credential redacted
assert(text.includes("https://api.acme.io/v2")); // non-credential survives
```

Step 3. Validate Fix 2, capping loses identifiers and recovery overflows:

```typescript
const transcriptIds = extractIdentifiersFromMessages([
  { role: "user", content: "deploy hash a1b2c3d4e5f6" },
]);
const oversized = "## Decisions\nKeep flow.\n## Exact identifiers\n"
  + "x".repeat(MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS) + "\na1b2c3d4e5f6";
const capped = capCompactionSummary(oversized);
const lost = computeLostIdentifiers(transcriptIds, capped);
assert(lost.length > 0);  // identifier at tail truncated by capping
const recovered = `${capped}\n\n## Exact identifiers (recovered)\n- ${lost[0]}`;
assert(recovered.length > MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS); // recovery overflows
```

Step 4. Run `npx tsx proof-75336.ts` from the patched branch.

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ cd /tmp/wt-75336 && npx tsx proof-75336.ts

[Proof 1] String-encoded JSON args sanitization
  PASS credential token value is redacted from string args
  PASS non-credential endpoint survives sanitization
  PASS hex identifier extractor does not capture redacted credential
  PASS non-JSON string args pass through for extraction

[Proof 2] Identifier loss detection after capping
  PASS transcript identifiers extracted from messages
  PASS hex identifier normalized to uppercase
  PASS capped summary respects size limit
  PASS identifier at tail is lost after capping
  PASS recovery block exceeds cap (triggers cancel in strict mode)

Results: 9 passed, 0 failed
```

Observed result after fix:

Fix 1: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` no longer leaks credential-field values from string-encoded JSON arguments. The `token` field value `deadbeef12345678` is redacted by `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before the hex identifier extractor can capture it. Non-credential fields and non-JSON strings pass through normally.

Fix 2: When strict mode detects identifier loss after capping and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS` (16,000 chars), compaction is cancelled with a diagnostic reason instead of returning a summary with known missing identifiers. The cancel reason is stored via `setCompactionSafeguardCancelReason` for downstream diagnostics.

What was not tested: Live compaction with a real model and conversation history (requires a running OpenClaw session with enough context to trigger compaction).
